name: Herman Miller Aeron Chair
positives: Flexible back and twin lumbar support.
Configurable builds with multiple finishes and sizes.
Customizable options for style and lumbar support.
Smooth tilting mechanism with three locking positions.
12-year manufacturer warranty included.
Multi-layered seat with adjustable pressure zones.
User-friendly design.
Suspension mesh promotes clean, neutral postures.
Strong graphite frame for durability.
Best-in-class ultra-adaptive mesh upholstery.
Forward tilt supports active, forward-leaning postures.
negatives: Posturefit lumbar less effective than lumbar slider.
Non-adjustable seat depth.
Limited recline range: 93°, 102°, 113°.
Restrictive seat design: Hard edges limit foot placement.
Outdated 3D armrests lack modern adjustability.
Backrest tilts and rocks, no lock feature.
Neutral posture support and 12-year warranty.
Armrests only 3D adjustable.
Headrest sold separately.
Function over flair, minimal extras.
Hard edges on seat can annoy legs.

positives_detail: Flexible back and twin lumbar support offers continuous back support.
Configurable builds: Multiple frame finishes, lumbar options, and size variants are available.
Customizable builds (choose your style, lumbar support type, etc).
Incredibly smooth tilting mechanism with three locking positions.
Includes a 12-year manufacturer warranty for peace of mind.
Multi-layered seat with varying pressure zones that intelligently adjusts to the body.
Very easy to use.
Easy to use: The suspension mesh naturally guides users into clean, neutral postures.
Strong graphite frame ensures long-lasting durability.
Best in class ultra-adaptive mesh upholstery.
Forward tilt: One of the few chairs that allows a downward-sloping seat (supports active, forward-leaning postures).
negatives_detail: The flashy Posturefit lumbar isn’t as effective as the simple lumbar slider.
Seat depth isn’t adjustable.
Narrow recline range: The limited 93°, 102°, and 113° recline enforces perpetual upright work postures.
Restrictive seat design: Hard, bladed seat edges force users to sit with planted feet at all times.
Outdated armrests: 3D adjustability is limited versus modern multi-axis (4D–6D) designs.
Backrest only tilts and rocks but doesn’t lock into a reclined position.
Boring. The superb neutral posture support and 12-year warranty are the main selling points.
The armrests are only 3D adjustable.
Headrest is sold separately as an attachment.
Limited ‘wow’ factor: Delivers function over flair, with few experiential extras.
Hard plastic edges lining the seat can annoy your legs.

negatives_detail: Assembly instructions are overly convoluted.

Some people may find the seat firmness too high.
The Titan allows for more sitting freedom than the Aeron. This means it’s easier to adopt poor sitting habits in the Titan without conscious effort, compared to the Aeron.
